macro_summary: Thursday's session saw the S&P 500 slip 0.19% to 731.58 as chip stocks gave back some of this week's rally. The QQQ edged up 0.06% to 694.94. The FOMC decision released on May 7 held rates at 3.50%–3.75%, citing Middle East uncertainty per US Bank. WTI eased another 0.27% to $95.83 as the Hormuz de-escalation deepened, with Trump's pause holding. Gold dipped 0.16% to $4,720.50. The US10Y rose 4bp to 4.39%. Non-farm payrolls this morning are the next catalyst.
commodities_note: WTI slipped to $95.83, its third straight decline from the $105 spike, as Iran ceasefire talks continue. Gold at $4,720.50 remains elevated but momentum stalled. The DXY at 98.20 is essentially flat. No data anomalies detected on benchmarks.

singapore.summary: The Singapore bank cluster remains under dual headwinds from elevated US rates and MAS S$NEER appreciation. US10Y at 4.39% is still above the 4.20% threshold; MAS policy unchanged until October. OCBC reports today, which will be the key test of NIM resilience — OCBC has been the relative outperformer. UOB earns its first ACCUMULATE as technicals improve, but near-term NIM relief is not yet visible.
singapore.key_development: OCBC earnings today are the direct test of NIM trajectory; UOB up to ACCUMULATE on improved R:R despite lack of near-term catalyst.

semiconductors.summary: The semiconductor cluster is uniformly extended after this week's powerful rally. Eleven of 21 watchlist members are CAUTION, mostly extreme-extension blocks. NVDA, AMD, INTC, MU, SK Hynix, and others are all 20%+ above their 50-day averages — a pullback is necessary for any entry. AVGO's OpenAI financing snag adds a new risk to custom ASIC thesis, but the dominant theme is technical overextension, not thesis breakage.
semiconductors.key_development: Uniform CAUTION across semis; no buyable entries exist. AVGO dropped 2.7% on OpenAI chip financing roadblock, but the extension remains the primary block.

big_tech.summary: BigTech is split: MSFT holds at +5.8% above SMA50, while AMZN and GOOG are in extreme-extension territory (+18% and +23% respectively). PLTR remains CAUTION on valuation. The cluster needs a broader pullback to become actionable; AI capex narratives remain intact but prices have outrun fundamentals.
big_tech.thesis_status: Structurally intact, technically extended — wait for pullbacks
big_tech.key_development: MSFT's Azure quality debate persists; AMZN and GOOG are extended, with no near-term catalysts to break the block.

interconnected.entry_note: Monitor if RSI dips below 30 and forward P/E compresses under 20x.

active_risks: US-Iran Hormuz standoff: oil supply disruption risk persists despite de-escalation talks; WTI near $96.
US-China chip tariff escalation: Trump's new tariffs on Chinese chips (2027) add supply chain uncertainty.
Fed rate pause: inflation uncertainty from energy prices keeps rates elevated, US10Y at 4.39%.
China COMAC aviation sanctions: US revoked export licenses for C919 components, hindering China's jet ambitions.
geopolitical.market_impact: Oil easing to $95.83 is a tailwind for transport and consumer, but persistent above-trend WTI still pressures margins for semis (ASML, MU, TSM via logistics). Chip tariffs add long-term headwinds for foundry expansion (TSMC, Samsung). Defense spending surge benefits PLTR, but its valuation remains extended.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: FOMC held rates steady at 3.50%–3.75% (May 7 decision), as widely expected. No new escalation in Hormuz.
geopolitical.portfolio_action: Hold cash; no new entries until the US10Y breaks below 4.20% and semis cluster pull back to SMA50 zones.

portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: Seventeen of 21 names are hard-blocked on technical extension — the entire watchlist is overbought. No buyable entries exist; cash is king.

macro_trigger_map.bearish_outcome: Hot wages rekindle inflation fears, pushing US10Y above 4.50%.
